_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**1. Emissions Data Collection and Reporting Infrastructure**
The workspace must build or formalise a per-shipment emissions calculation capability aligned to National Greenhouse Accounts (NGA) factors. This is required to satisfy Scope 3 data requests from Group 1 clients (reporting from FY ending 30 June 2026). Scope: all transport modes managed — air freight (highest priority), road drayage, and ocean consolidations on Americas/Europe/Asia trade lanes. Gap from baseline: most freight forwarders currently capture distance and weight but not CO₂-e per consignment at the transport-leg level. Required fields: origin/destination, mode, carrier fuel type, distance (km), load factor, emission factor (NGA-sourced), CO₂-e result, data vintage. Target: operational by Q4 2025 at latest; ideally immediately given first Group 1 reports are due FY2025–26.

*Source: Navigating new transport carbon reporting requirements 2025, cario.com.au. https://cario.com.au/resources/navigating-the-new-transport-carbon-reporting-requirements-in-2025. Upcoming Carbon Emission Reporting in Transport, deliver.com.au. https://www.deliver.com.au/industry-trends/what-australias-new-emissions-reporting-rules-mean-for-freight-customers-and-how-deliver-can-assist*

**2. Supplier/Carrier Emissions Data Onboarding**
The workspace must formalise carbon data requests in carrier and subcontractor contracts — particularly for road hauliers used in live events and film/TV production logistics where short-notice charter and ad hoc carriers are common. Each carrier should supply: vehicle/aircraft type, fuel type, average emission factor, and willingness to provide actual fuel consumption data per booking. Scope: all contracted carriers across AU domestic legs and international feeder routes. Deadline: formalise in new and renewing contracts from mid-2025. Gap: ad hoc carriers typically have no formal emissions disclosure capability.

*Source: cario.com.au, ibid.*

**3. Audit-Ready Record Retention**
The workspace must retain emissions records for a minimum of five years to meet assurance requirements under the ASRS/Corporations Act regime. Records must be in a format accessible to financial statement auditors. Scope: all consignment-level CO₂-e records, methodology references (NGA factor version used), and carrier data sources. Gap: standard freight management systems do not typically retain sustainability data at consignment level with audit provenance.

*Source: cario.com.au, ibid. Allens mandatory climate reporting guidance, November 2025. https://www.allens.com.au/insights-news/insights/2024/09/mandatory-climate-related-financial-reporting-legislation/*

**4. ACCU/Offset Product Controls**
If the workspace offers carbon-neutral freight products (relevant for luxury goods, fine art, and humanitarian verticals where clients may seek certified-neutral shipments), internal controls must be established to ensure ACCU scheme integrity. Given the CCA's 2023 finding that non-carbon benefit reporting in the ACCU scheme is unregulated and greenwashing risk exists, the workspace must apply additional due diligence before marketing offset-backed freight products. Scope: any client-facing green freight product. Deadline: before any new product launch. Gap: no standard industry due-diligence checklist currently exists for forwarder ACCU procurement.

*Source: 2023 Review of the Carbon Credits Act 2011, Climate Change Authority, 2023. https://www.climatechangeauthority.gov.au/sites/default/files/documents/2023-12/2023%20Review%20of%20the%20Carbon%20Credits%20Act%202011%20-%20publication.pdf*

**5. Staff Training**
Staff responsible for key account management, trade compliance, and operations on Australian trade lanes must understand the CCA's advisory role, the NGER/ASRS interface, and the workspace's obligations as a Scope 3 data supplier. Training scope: one briefing cycle covering the CCA governance architecture, NGER thresholds, ASRS Scope 3 Category 4 methodology, and ACCU scheme basics. Recommended delivery: before end of FY2025–26.
